mnemonics: VIP CPU: Virtualization Isolates Physical CPU
MIMIC: Memory Isolation Means Independent Containers
keyPoints: CPU Virtualization allows multiple VMs to share a single physical processor by time-slicing and presenting virtual CPUs.
Memory Virtualization uses a Memory Management Unit (MMU) to map physical memory to isolated virtual address spaces for each VM.
I/O Virtualization uses hypervisor-based emulation or direct device passthrough (like SR-IOV) to give VMs controlled access to hardware.
commonMistakes: Assuming virtualized resources perform identically to dedicated physical hardware (there is overhead).
Believing that complete isolation between VMs is always guaranteed (vulnerabilities like side-channel attacks can exist).
